Course Overview
Initial content covered in Week 1.

BM25
Content covered in Week 2.

Boolean Retrieval
Topic covered during Week 1.

TF-IDF
Topic covered during Week 2.

Link Analysis
Content covered in Week 3.

Week 1
The first week of the course schedule.

Vector Space Model
Topic covered during Week 2.

The Course
A course with defined grading components, academic policies, and a schedule for topics and events.

IR Book
A textbook available online at https://nlp.stanford.edu/IR-book/, relevant to the course content.

This Course Homework Grade Component
This component accounts for 30% of the final grade, consisting of five programming assignments (2%, 7%, 7%, 7%, 7%).

This Course Grading Policy
Defines the grading scale for the course: A (>=90%), B (>=80% and <90%), C (>=70% and <80%), D (>=60% and <70%), F (<60%).

This Course Homework 1
The first programming assignment, designed to introduce students to Jupyter Notebook and common Python operations used in the course.

This Course Programming Assignments
Five programming assignments that are individual, written in Python, and collectively contribute to the Homework grade component.

Week 2
The second week of the course schedule.

Python
A programming language used for all programming assignments in the course.

Jupyter Notebook
A computing environment used in the first programming assignment for familiarization with Python operations.

This Course Quizzes Grade Component
This component accounts for 20% of the final grade, consisting of four brief in-class quizzes (5% each).

This Course Final Exam Schedule Event

data.entity_name: This Course Final Exam Grade Component
data.content:
This Course Final Exam Grade Component
This component accounts for 30% of the final grade, based on a comprehensive final exam.

This Course Final Exam
A comprehensive final exam scheduled from 3:30 PM to 5:30 PM on May 4 in HRBB 113.

HRBB 113
The location where the comprehensive final exam for the course will be held.

May 4
The date on which the comprehensive final exam for the course will be held.

data.entity_name: This Course Group Project Grade Component
data.content:
This Course Group Project Grade Component
This component accounts for 20% of the final grade, based on a mini-research project completed in teams of 3 or 4

CSCE 670
CSCE 670 is a course titled "Information Storage and Retrieval" offered in Spring 2026, Section 600 (46627), at College Station, carrying 3 credit hours.

Yu Zhang
Yu Zhang is the instructor for CSCE 670, with an office located at PETR 222, email yuzhang@tamu.edu, and phone (217) 954-3457.

CSCE 670 Meeting Schedule
The meeting schedule for CSCE 670, occurring MWF from 1:50 PM to 2:40 PM, starting on 01/12/2026 and ending on 05/05/2026, located in HRBB 113.

Information Storage And Retrieval
This topic covers the representation, storage, and access to very large multimedia document collections, fundamental data structures, algorithms, and techniques to design and evaluate retrieval systems.

This Course In-Class Quizzes
Four brief in-class quizzes, each contributing 5% to the final grade.
